summary: Gruber reflects on Steve Jobs's resignation as Apple CEO, acknowledging that while it wasn't unexpected given Jobs's medical leave, it still felt like a shock. He argues that Apple under Tim Cook is functionally the same company it was the day before, since Cook had already been running operations for months. Gruber emphasizes that Jobs's greatest achievement was building Apple itself as an institution — a 'fractal design' where the company's values permeate every level. The resignation is framed as one step in a long-planned, orderly transition that Jobs orchestrated with the same care he brought to products.
key_points: The announcement was not unexpected but still emotionally shocking — a distinction Gruber initially resisted but came to accept
Tim Cook had already been functioning as chief executive for months; the title change merely formalized reality
Apple's succession plan was deliberate and methodical, with lieutenants like Schiller, Forstall, and Cue given increasingly prominent roles
Jobs's greatest creation is Apple itself — the company as an institution that embodies his values at every level
The company is described as a 'fractal design' where simplicity, elegance, and attention to detail repeat at every scale
money_quotes: This is not out of nowhere, it's not even unexpected. We could all see this was coming — but it is a shock.
Apple tomorrow, a week from now, and next month is the exact same Apple from yesterday, a week ago, and last month.
The company is a fractal design. Simplicity, elegance, beauty, cleverness, humility. Directness. Truth.
Jobs's greatest creation isn't any Apple product. It is Apple itself.

The same thought, care, and painstaking attention to detail that Steve Jobs brought to questions like 'How should a computer work?' he also brought to the most important question: 'How should a company that creates such things function?'
Today's announcement is just one more step, albeit a big and sad one, in a long-planned orderly transition — a transition that no one wanted but which could not, alas, be avoided.
He's here, but this is the first post-Steve keynote.
key_insight: Jobs's most lasting achievement was designing Apple itself as an institution that could embody his values and carry on without him.
